def: n. m. جـﮩر was conspicuous. 1. A jewel; gem. 2. Virtue; worth; merit; excellence; beauty. 3. Secrets. Sharāb hai ye, samajh-ke pīnā, kharāb kahtā hai isko ālam, Kahīṅ nashe meṅ khuleṅ na jauhar, idhar hamāre, udhar tumhāre . Jauhar. In moderation drink—a bad name hath this wine, Lest drink our secret but expose, there yours, here mine. 4. Nature; substance; constitution; essence. 5. ( Logic ) An absolute or essential property, as distinguished from the accidental ( ٴarz ). 6. Spirit; faculty; understanding; penetration. 7. Skill; knowledge; art; accomplishment; essence. 8. The temper or water of steel. jauhar dikhānā , v. n. To show to advantage. Khūb jauhar dikhāë . She showed to great advantage. jauhar k. , v. n. ( Obs .) To kill one's self; to commit suicide. ☞ When the Rajputs are attacked by an overwhelming force they sometimes slaughter or burn their wives and children, and then sell their lives dearly on the field of battle. jauhar khulnā , v. n. 1. To shine (one's abilities). 2. To be exposed (one's faults.) ahl-i-jauhar , Men of merit or ability. Ahl-i-jauhar ko vatan meṅ rahne detā gar falak , Zauq. Lāl kyoṅ is raṅg se ātā Badakhshāṅ chhoṛ-kar? Did Heaven permit good men in their own native land to stay, Say would the ruby from Badakhshāṅ e'er have come away?
more: n. f. S. युज to join. A yoke attached to a plough.

def: s.m.f. A gem, jewel; a pearl; essence, matter, substance, constituent, material part (opp. to accident), absolute or essential property; skill, knowledge, accomplishment, art; excellence, worth, merit, virtue; secret nature; defects, vices; — the diversified wavy marks, streaks, or grain of a well-tempered sword; — adj. Bright, shining, glittering
more: (from Pers. گوهر) s. m. f. 1. A gem, jewel, pearl. 2. Skill, knowledge. 3. Matter, substance, essence (distinguished from accident). 4. Virtue, worth, merit. 5. Blackish marks in the steel of well tempered scimitars. 6. adj. Bright, glittering. جوهردار jauhar-dār, A scimitar marked with jauhar, q. v. جوهرکرنا jauhar karnā, To kill oneʼs self, to commit suicide. (When the Hindūs are attacked by a force too great for them to resist, they sometimes, in such cases, slaughter or burn their own wives and children, and then take to flight, or are themselves slain; and, this act of killing their wives, &c. in such extremities, they call jūhar or jauhar ).
subs: adj. & s.m. Marked with jauhar; — a scimitar so marked

def: s.m. Answer, reply, response (to a letter, writing, saying, or question); retort, rejoinder; anything answering to another, counterpart, facsimile, double, pair; return, reward, compensation; discharge, dismissal, congé; refusal (of an offer of marriage, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Counterpart; correspondent part; double; pair. 2. Answer; reply; retort; rejoinder; defence. 1. Savāl kā javāb hai . 2. Yāṅ lab pe lākh lākh sakhun iztirāb meṅ ; Vān ek khāmushī tere sab ke javāb meṅ . Zauq. A hundred thousand words are on my restless lips, With her it's silence mute, no word from her e'er slips. 3. Reward; compensation ( ajar ). Is kā javāb Khudā de . Wom. May God requite you for this! 4. Discharge; dismissal. 5. Refusal of an offer of marriage. javāb-ul-javāb , n. m. The rejoinder or reply. javāb bā-savāb , n. m. A good or proper answer. javāb-dāvā , n. m. The answer to a plaint, charge, or accusation. javāb-deh , adj. Answerable; responsible; accountable. javāb-deh , n. m. A defendant; respondent. javāb-dehī , n. f. Liability; responsibility. javāb-dehī se barī, yā barī-ul-zimmā k. , v. a. To relieve or discharge from liabilities; to exonerate. javāb-dehī se …

s. m. An answer, reply, any thing answering to another. جواب باصواب jawāb-i-bā-ṣawāb, s. m. A proper answer. جواب بالا jawāb-bālā, A kind of benediction (i. e. may your advice prevail). جواب ده jawāb-dih, Answerer; adj. Amenable, accountable, responsible, answerable. جواب دينا jawāb-denā, 1. To be accountable for, to be amenable or responsible, to reply, to answer, to give an answer or account (of any commission, &c.). 2. To discharge, disband, to dismiss one from his office. جواب سوال jawāb suwāl, s. m. Altercation, conversation. جواب سوالي jawāb-suwāl-ī, s. m. Reply, rejoinder. جواب قطعي jawāb-i-ḳat̤ʼīﻌ, s. m. A definitive answer. جواب ملنا jawāb milnā, To be dismissed or discharged. جوات نامه jawāb-nāma, s. m. A writing fixed to the winding sheet, on the breast of the deceased, which it is intended that Munkar and Nakīr should read, in case the poor soul should be too much flurried to…
subs: s.m. The rejoinder or reply to an answer; rejoinder
s.m. A fitting or proper answer
'May your advice prevail,' a kind of benediction
To get (one's) discharge or dismissal, receive (one's) congé; — jawāb-děh, adj. & s.m. Answerable, responsible, amenable, accountable; — answerer; one called to account; a respondent, defendant
s.f. The giving an answer, answering; the being responsible, responsibility, liability; the defence (in a court of law)
(-ko), To relieve of responsibility; to exonerate

adj. & s.m. Ready at reply or rejoinder; — one who is ready at rejoinder; — reply, rejoinder
s.m. (Gram.) An apodosis, the complement or correlative of a condition

adj. Requiring an answer; called to account

s.m. A definite or conclusive answer
(-kā), To make answer; to be responsible (for); to respond, to defend (a suit)
v.n. To be answered, to be silenced
v.n. To be discharged or dismissed ( = jawāb pānā)

s.m. A writing fixed to the winding-sheet, on the breast of the deceased, which it is intended that the angels Munkar and Nakīr (q.v.) should read in case the poor soul should be too much flurried to answer their questions
v.n. To be the counterpart (of, -kā), to correspond or agree (with); to be dismissed or discharged
s.m. A responsive or replicative particle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَوَاب (jawāb), from Arabic جَوَاب (jawāb).

def: adj. & s.m. Responsive; in reply; counter; re-; — counterpart; a respondent, defendant; — a kind of bill of exchange, which is not paid till notice is received of the bill's having been taken up; — the chorus (especially in repeating a mars̤iya or elegy)
more: (from جواب) s. m. 1. A respondent. 2. A kind of bill of exchange, the price of which is not paid till notice is received of the billʼs having been cashed. 3. The chorus, especially in repeating a mars̤iya or elegy. جوابي سوالي jawābī-sawālī, s. m. A mediator, an ambassadour, an agent, an attorney.

adj. Ornamented or bedecked with jewels, jewelled, studed with jewels.

def: n. m. pl. of جوﮩر Jewels; gems; precious stones. Juz jauharī kyā jāne koī qadr-i-javāhar? Samjhe hai sukhan-ras hī sakhun merī zabāṅ kā . Saudā. Except the jeweller who knows the worth of jewels rare? A master only can my poems' merits true declare. javāharāt' , n. m. Rich jewels; precious gems. Luṭ rahā hai javāharāt kā ḍher, Ab bhī koī na le to hai andher. javāhir-khānā , n. m. A store-house for jewels. javāhir-nigār adj. Jewelled; studded with jewels.
s.m. pl. (of jawāhir), Jewels, gems, &c.

def: s.f. Youth; adolescence, manhood, age of discretion, puberty; the season of youth
more: n. f. 1. Youth; manhood; adolescence; prime. 2. Puberty; the age of discretion; full age. 1. Ilāhī! bachche, tū apnī javānī se phal na pāë! Mah. Wom. (A curse). God grant you may never enjoy the pleasures of youth! 2. Javānī divānī . Prov. The glad time of youth is a mad time in sooth. 3. Naī javānī, māṅjhā ḍhīlā . Prov. Young and unstrung! (Indolence in the young). 4. Chām purānā, man nayā, nainan wohī subhāo, Arī javānī bāvrī, ek bār phir āo! Dohā. A wrinkled skin, the heart still fresh, the eyes their light retain! O wild youth gone from me, wouldst thou but come back once again! 5. Kuchh tujhī par to anokhī javānī nahīṅ chaṛhī! Wom. Not on you alone youth's rounded charms have newly burst! 6. Gaī javānī phir nā bahure chāhe lākh malīdā khāo . Prov. Youth gone will ne'er come back though nutriment no end you pack. javānī kā phal , n. m. The joys of youth. javānī kā ālam ; H. tarun ava…
subs: (-par), To attain to the vigour of manhood, to arrive at the age of puberty; to be affected by youthful lust

wety: From Prakrit *𑀛𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀟 (*jhaggaḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), a reconstructed variant of 𑀛𑀕𑀟 (jhagaḍa). See झगड़ना (jhagaṛnā) for more. Cognates Cognate with Punjabi ਝਗੜਾ / جھَگْڑَا (jhagṛā), Sindhi جَهڳڙِو (jhaɠṛo), جهيڙو (jheṛo), Bengali ঝগড়া (jhogoṛa), Assamese জগৰ (zogor).

def: n. m. See بکهيرَا 1—8, 12. Wrangling; strife; quarrel; brawl. 1. Lage ragṛā, miṭe jhagṛā . Bhangaṛs. If you begin to pound strife will cease to sound. (Let the toper have his glass, his ill-temper will pass.) 2. Bāp beṭā ke jhagṛā na phāve . Bhoj. Prov. For father and son to fight's not pleasing to the sight. 3. Rūs, Rūm kā jhagṛā paise ko! Delhi street cry. 4. Sab jīte jī ke jhagṛe haiṅ, sach pūchho to kyā khāk huē, Jab maut se-ā-kar kām paṛā sab qisse qazye pāk huë . Nazīr. All life is one long strife, the end whereof is only dust, When death the arbiter appears the strife is ended just. jhagṛā pāk honā, yā chuknā , 1. To be settled (a quarrel). 2. To die. jhagṛā karānā, yā uṭhānā , v. a. To make a quarrel; sow dissension; foment discord. binā jhagṛe , adj. Undisputed.
s.f. A quarrelsome or contentious woman.
more: f. adj. Quarrelsome, wrangling, disputatious, contentious; a brawler, an arguer.

def: adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, wrangling, disputatious, contentious, litigious; — a disputer, an arguer; a contentious or quarrelsome person, a brawler.

def: adj. Quarrelsome; litigious; wrangling. Muë jhagṛālū se kaun saudā kharīde? Mah. Wom.
v.t. To cause to quarrel, to set a-wrangling, to set by the ears, to get up a fight (between = jhagṛā karānā).
more: v. a. To set a wrangling.

def: A child's frock or coat (reaching to the feet); a child's angarkhā.
